---
title: ""
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image: images/portrait_2.jpg
  links:
    - icon: book
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=3o05O7kAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
    - icon: bank
      text: Arizona State University
      href: https://search.asu.edu/profile/3723615
---


:::{#hero-heading}
## Short Bio

Dr. Linton is an Assistant Professor of Women and Gender Studies in the [School of Social Transformation](https://search.asu.edu/profile/3723615) at Arizona State University.
 
Her first book is [now available for pre-order](https://uapress.arizona.edu/book/el-salvador-reborn). Published by the University of Arizona Press, the book is titled *El Salvador Reborn: Abortion, Environment and the Politics of Survival in a Postwar Nation*.

She received her Ph.D. in Ethnic Studies from UC San Diego, and her B.A. in English Literature and American Studies & Ethnicity from the University of Southern California. 

At Arizona State University, she is the faculty coordinator for the [Undergraduate LGBT Studies Certificate](https://degrees.apps.asu.edu/minors/major/ASU00/PPLGBTCERT/lesbian-gay-bisexual-and-transgender-studies), a faculty affiliate with the [School of Transborder Studies](https://sts.asu.edu/), and a faculty advisor in Barrett the Honors College. 
:::
